Subject: [Crm114-announce] crm114 filters all mails to unsure ..
Body: Hello I have just compiled crm114, there was no problem visible. A test on the commandline was ok: > X-CRM114-Version: 20070301-BlameBaltar ( TRE 0.7.5 (LGPL) ) MR-BD9991E2 > X-CRM114-CacheID: sfid-20070328_230929_066681_D47F7285 > X-CRM114-Status: UNSURE ( 0.00 ) > X-CRM114-Notice: Please train this message. But wenn I setup the filters in KMail I get all mails turned back as UNSURE. I use this to check my mails: crm -u $HOME/.crm114 mailreaver.crm To move the unsure mails to spam, if they are, I use: crm -u $HOME/.crm114 mailreaver.crm --spam To move the unsure mails to ham, if they are, I use: crm -u $HOME/.crm114 mailreaver.crm --good But when I then send the same mails though the same filters again, they are still detected as UNSURE Guess I need some help at this point. I assume that I have somewhere a problem in my setup.. Thanks a lot Michael -- "Feel free" - 10 GB Mailbox, 100 FreeSMS/Monat ... Hello I have just compiled crm114, there was no problem visible. A test on the commandline was ok: > X-CRM114-Version: 20070301-BlameBaltar ( TRE 0.7.5 (LGPL) ) MR-BD9991E2 > X-CRM114-CacheID: sfid-20070328_230929_066681_D47F7285 > X-CRM114-Status: UNSURE ( 0.00 ) > X-CRM114-Notice: Please train this message. But wenn I setup the filters in KMail I get all mails turned back as UNSURE. I use this to check my mails: crm -u $HOME/.crm114 mailreaver.crm To move the unsure mails to spam, if they are, I use: crm -u $HOME/.crm114 mailreaver.crm --spam To move the unsure mails to ham, if they are, I use: crm -u $HOME/.crm114 mailreaver.crm --good But when I then send the same mails though the same filters again, they are still detected as UNSURE Guess I need some help at this point. Subject: Re: [opensuse] Power Supply PSU Efficiency (was: Unstable system - who is the culprit?
Body: On Saturday 09 February 2008 09:39:13 am Randall R Schulz wrote: > On Saturday 09 February 2008 11:22, Philippe Landau wrote: > > Randall R Schulz wrote: > > > On Saturday 09 February 2008 10:08, Philippe Landau wrote: > > >> But many power supplies are only efficient when > > >> they don't operate close to full load. > > > > > > This seemed counterintuitive to me, so I decided to do a little > > > looking around. According to the graph on page 14 of "Power > > > Supplies: A Hidden Opportunity for Energy Savings" > > > > > > this is not the case. > > > > It very much depends on the power supply in question. > > Of course. > > But to go back to the original question (mine), the issue is whether > highly over-rated supply is wasteful of power. It appears they are. > > > Randall Schulz I disagree: I looked at a bunch of efficiency plots from 80plus.org. They generally peak at 50% of the rated load, at around 84-85%. their low load (20%) efficiency is around 82%, the high load (100%) efficiency is around 80%. so, for a 400 watt load, typical i would say for today's computers, an 800 watt power supply would draw around 470 watts, as it would operate at 85% efficiency. for the same 400 watt load a 400 watt power supply would draw 500 watts, efficiency at 80% for the same load, a 550 w power supply would draw around 488 watts, efficiencyaround 82% for the typical efficiency curve shown on this site, the same 400 watt load, supplied by a 2000 watt power supply , would draw 488 watts, as at 20% load the efficiency is at 82%. so, it is better to get a power supply at least twice as big as the load. Even when the rating is way greater, say 4 times the requirement, the efficiency is better than using a power supply at over 75% of it's capacity. the SUV syndrome fails when applied to power supplies:) d.

On Saturday 09 February 2008 09:39:13 am Randall R Schulz wrote: > On Saturday 09 February 2008 11:22, Philippe Landau wrote: > > Randall R Schulz wrote: > > > On Saturday 09 February 2008 10:08, Philippe Landau wrote: > > >> But many power supplies are only efficient when > > >> they don't operate close to full load. > > > > > > This seemed counterintuitive to me, so I decided to do a little > > > looking around. According to the graph on page 14 of "Power > > > Supplies: A Hidden Opportunity for Energy Savings" > > > > > > this is not the case. > > > > It very much depends on the power supply in question. > > Of course. > > But to go back to the original question (mine), the issue is whether > highly over-rated supply is wasteful of power. It appears they are. > > > Randall Schulz I disagree: I looked at a bunch of efficiency plots from 80plus.org. They generally peak at 50% of the rated load, at around 84-85%. their low load (20%) efficiency is around 82%, the high load (100%) efficiency is around 80%. so, for a 400 watt load, typical i would say for today's computers, an 800 watt power supply would draw around 470 watts, as it would operate at 85% efficiency. for the same 400 watt load a 400 watt power supply would draw 500 watts, efficiency at 80% for the same load, a 550 w power supply would draw around 488 watts, efficiencyaround 82% for the typical efficiency curve shown on this site, the same 400 watt load, supplied by a 2000 watt power supply , would draw 488 watts, as at 20% load the efficiency is at 82%. so, it is better to get a power supply at least twice as big as the load. Even when the rating is way greater, say 4 times the requirement, the efficiency is better than using a power supply at over 75% of it's capacity. the SUV syndrome fails when applied to power supplies:) d.

Subject: [Spambayes-checkins] spambayes README.txt,1.66,1.67
Body: Update of /cvsroot/spambayes/spambayes In directory sc8-pr-cvs8.sourceforge.net:/tmp/cvs-serv7047 Modified Files: README.txt Log Message: Add some directions about starting the XML-RPC (core) server. Index: README.txt =================================================================== RCS file: /cvsroot/spambayes/spambayes/README.txt,v retrieving revision 1.66 retrieving revision 1.67 diff -C2 -d -r1.66 -r1.67 *** README.txt 12 Apr 2004 01:59:26 -0000 1.66 --- README.txt 27 Jun 2007 01:59:49 -0000 1.67 *************** *** 179,182 **** --- 179,253 ---- + XML-RPC Server + -------------- + + The XML-RPC server (new in 1.1a4) web interface is almost identical the the + POP3 proxy user interface. Instead of proxying POP3 communications though + it provides an XML-RPC server your (typically non-mail) applications can use + to score content submissions. + + To install and configure it: + + 1. Unpack and install the distribution: + + tar xvfz spambayes-1.1a4.tar.gz + cd spambayes-1.1a4 + python setup.py install + + 2. Devote a runtime directory to it: + + SBDIR=/usr/local/spambayes/core_server # or whatever... + mkdir -p $SBDIR + + 3. Create an INI file: + + cd $SBDIR + cat > bayescustomize.ini <<EOF + [globals] + verbose:False + + [Headers] + include_evidence:True + include_score:True + + [Tokenizer] + record_header_absence:True + summarize_email_prefixes:True + summarize_email_suffixes:True + mine_received_headers:True + x-pick_apart_urls:True + x-fancy_url_recognition:False + x-lookup_ip:True + lookup_ip_cache:$SBDIR/dnscache.pck + max_image_size:100000 + crack_image_cache:$SBDIR/imagecache.pck + + crack_images:True + image_size:True + ocr_engine:gocr + [Classifier] + use_bigrams:False + + [Categorization] + ham_cutoff:0.2 + spam_cutoff:0.85 + + [Storage] + persistent_storage_file:$SBDIR/hammie.db + persistent_use_database:pickle + messageinfo_storage_file:$SBDIR/messageinfo.fs + + [html_ui] + display_score:True + EOF + + 4. Finally, start it: + + BAYESCUSTOMIZE=$SBDIR/bayescustomize.ini core_server.py -m XMLRPCPlugin + + Note that it creates both a web server (defaulting to localhost:8880) and an + XML-RPC server (defaulting to localhost:8001). + + Procmail filtering ------------------ _______________________________________________ Spambayes-checkins mailing list pnperxdpv-sarswdlp@python.org http://mail.python.org/mailman/listinfo/spambayes-checkins

Finally, start it: + + BAYESCUSTOMIZE=$SBDIR/bayescustomize.ini core_server.py -m XMLRPCPlugin + + Note that it creates both a web server (defaulting to localhost:8880) and an + XML-RPC server (defaulting to localhost:8001). + + Procmail filtering ------------------ _______________________________________________ Spambayes-checkins mailing list pnperxdpv-sarswdlp@python.org http://mail.python.org/mailman/listinfo/spambayes-checkins

Subject: Er du klar for uimotstГҘelig glГёd i vГҘr og sommer? вӯҗ
Body: NETTSIDEN Er du klar for uimotstГҘelig glГёd i vГҘr og sommer? вңЁ VГҘren og sommeren er tiden for frisk, strГҘlende hud! Derfor har vi satt sammen vГҘre mest populГҰre behandlinger som gir deg: вҳҖпёҸJevn hudoverflate вҳҖпёҸ Гҳkt kollagenproduksjon вҳҖпёҸIntens nГҰring og fukt вҳҖпёҸNaturlig, sunn glГёd La oss introdusere deg for dine nye favoritter: SOMMERGLГҳD En rask, men svГҰrt effektiv behandling som gir huden din en umiddelbar oppfriskning. Kombinasjonen av peeling, aktiv maske og LED-lys gir huden ny energi og synlig glГёd. Perfekt for deg som Гёnsker rask effekt fГёr sommerens begivenheter! п‘ү UtfГёres av vГҘre dyktige hudterapeuter. SOMMERGLГҳD+ Vil du ha det lille ekstra? SommerglГёd+ inkluderer alt fra SommerglГёd, men gir i tillegg en dyptvirkende mesoterapi som booster fukt, reduserer fine linjer og stimulerer kollagenproduksjonen. Behandlingen for deg som vil ha bГҘde glГёd og langvarig hudforbedring. п‘ү UtfГёres av vГҘre kosmetiske sykepleiere Resultatet: StrГҘlende, frisk og jevn hud Reduserte fine linjer Fornyet spenst og elastisitet En fГёlelse av velvГҰre og overskudd SJEKK KAMPANJENE п”Һ Hva skjer under behandlingen? 1. Peeling вҖ“ SkГҘnsom eksfoliering som fjerner dГёde hudceller og gir en jevn hudoverflate. 2. Mesoterapi (kun i SommerglГёd+) вҖ“ Dyp nГҰring og fuktighet med collagen-boost. 3. Maske вҖ“ TilfГёrer intensiv fukt og nГҰring for en frisk glГёd. 4. LED-lysbehandling вҖ“ Roer ned huden, stimulerer cellefornyelse og fremmer en sunn, strГҘlende hud. Book her Usikker pГҘ hva du skal velge? Ring oss pГҘ п“һ 380 25090 вҖ“ vi hjelper deg gjerne ГҘ finne riktig behandling for dine behov. п“Қ Velkommen til Renates Dagspa вҖ“ vi gleder oss til ГҘ se deg! facebook instagram Edit preferences | Unsubscribe

Subject: Re: Forwarding all localmail
Body: Randy Ramsdell wrote: > I would like to know what would be better that using aliases or > .forward to forward all local mail. I want to simply forward all mail > without having to intervene for new users or new services. > Any suggestion? remove the domain from mydestination and add it to relay_domains. then configure your transports or your DNS to route such mail to the new server.
text:
Re: Forwarding all localmail

Randy Ramsdell wrote: > I would like to know what would be better that using aliases or > .forward to forward all local mail. I want to simply forward all mail > without having to intervene for new users or new services. > Any suggestion? remove the domain from mydestination and add it to relay_domains. then configure your transports or your DNS to route such mail to the new server.

The Best Walking Sandals For Women, According To Our Editor Who Wore Them All Walking sandals tend to be better cushioned to absorb impact and cradle your foot with more built-in arch support that takes pressure off your heels and prevents pain. Many are also washable, water-resistant and have straps to hold your foot securely in place. Some are even stylish enough to wear to dinner. Fashion editor Kari Molvar laid out all her tried-and-true favorites, so you can be comfortable wherever the summer takes you. Read The Story Our Top Picks Teva Hurricane XLT 2 TevaвҖҷs Hurricane sandals are a hiking classicвҖ” and this new version is made from recycled materials and features a more supportive midsole. They're comfortable right out of the box, with the right amount of cushy arch support and three places to adjust the straps (over the toes, foot and back of the heel).$72 From Amazon Hoka Ora Luxe The updated version of the bestselling Ora recovery sandal has two adjustable straps and all the comfort and support as the original. Hoka is famously known for their curved and contoured rocker sole, which these sandals are outfitted with, and the fit includes plenty of space for toes to spread out.$80 From Zappos Mephisto Helen Sandals Mephistos are often considered the gold standard in comfortable, high-end leather sandals that are also sophisticated. Devotees say say the contoured footbed is superior to Birkenstocks and the design is more sophisticated. They come in a range of colors, so theyвҖҷre a versatile and stylish option for everyday and travel.$107 From Amazon Sorel Viibe Sandal For chic yet supportive sandals, Sorel makes great options. Their newest Viibe puffy leather sandals bring an element of fun to super-functional footwear, and theyвҖҷre also lightweight with straps that adjust to your foot width. As an added bonus, they come in a variety of on-trend colors.$125 From Sorel PRESENTED BY: Get Dresses For Wedding Season At Windsor Windsor offers a range of wedding guest dresses that are as eye-catching as they are affordable. Here's why this budget-friendly sports bra is worth every penny. More From Forbes These Are The 36 Best Sales Online Right Now We've rounded up the best sales onlineвҖ”from clothing to luxury beauty products to popular tech and moreвҖ”happening right now. Subject: Tour openers
Body: Meet the artists who will be performing with Little Moon across the country View this email online Welcome to the Tiny Desk Contest community! This newsletter is where we share Contest updates: advice from judges, entries weвҖҷre loving, winner announcements and more. You can expect to hear from us weekly during the Contest season, and monthly when weвҖҷre in-between Contests. WeвҖҷre glad youвҖҷre here, NPRвҖҷs Tiny Desk Contest team What a week! Since we announced Little Moon as this yearвҖҷs Contest winner last Tuesday, the band has been kicking into gear, preparing for its Tiny Desk concert and tour. Hours after the big announcement, singer-guitarist Emma and bassist Nathan Hardyman spoke with NPRвҖҷs Ari Shapiro on All Things Considered. They shared more about the personal story behind their winning song, being вҖңused to not winningвҖқ and what it feels like to now have won the Contest after entering four times. The band members and Bob Boilen also spoke with Utah NPR Member station KUER last week. And if that wasnвҖҷt enough, Little Moon somehow found time to release a studio version of its winning song, вҖңWonder Eye,вҖқ and an official music video.Next month, the band will be hitting the road to headline our Tiny Desk Contest On The Road tour. Today, weвҖҷre excited to share which local Contest entrants will opening at those stops: Los Angeles: alegrГӯas and Anilee List San Francisco: Asher Jaffe and Flaco el Jandro y Los Perros Callejeros Seattle: La Fonda and LIVt Philadelphia: Julia Pratt and Kikashima Chicago: Ephraim Bugumba and Nola AdГ© Houston: Cure For Paranoia and Micah EdwardsThe 2023 Tiny Desk Contest is presented by Capital One and supported by GuayakГӯ Yerba Mate. Newsletter continues after sponsor message Our Contest judges loved each of these artistsвҖҷ entries вҖ” a handful of them have been featured on Top Shelf вҖ” and we know theyвҖҷll put on great live performances. Tickets to these shows are selling quickly, so be sure to get yours soon at NPRPresents.org. We canвҖҷt wait to celebrate with you!From the Desk, NPRвҖҷs Tiny Desk Contest team Listen to your local NPR station. Visit NPR.org to hear live radio from KUNC (edit station). What do you think of today's email? We'd love to hear your thoughts, questions and feedback: tinydeskcontest@npr.orgInterested in connecting with music-lovers and music-makers across the Contest community? Join our Tiny Desk Contest Community Facebook Group!Need a new playlist? Follow NPR Music on Spotify and Apple Music!Looking for more great content? Check out all of our newsletter offerings вҖ” including Music, Books, Pop Culture and more! You received this message because you're subscribed to Tiny Desk Contest emails. This email was sent by National Public Radio, Inc., 1111 North Capitol Street NE, Washington, DC 20002Unsubscribe | Privacy Policy
